sourceMovie.title: Moonlight
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Moonlight for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Barry Jenkins keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Moonlight clicked for you, The Perks of Being a Wallflower (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Moonlight?
faq.answer: Try The Perks of Being a Wallflower, Requiem for a Dream, Love, Simon—each shares DNA with Moonlight while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Moonlight?
faq.answer: Moonlight (2016) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Moonlight worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
